title: Sorbus zolyomii
taxonomy: Sorbus zolyomii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, native to Hungary. It belongs to the genus Sorbus, which includes several species of rowans and whitebeams. The plant is a deciduous shrub or small tree with white flowers and red berries. As a member of the Rosaceae family, it is closely related to other plants such as apples, pears, and cherries. Sorbus zolyomii is considered an endemic species, meaning it is found only in its native region of Hungary.

side_effects: Sorbus zolyomii is a species of rowan tree native to Hungary, and there isn't much information available about its side effects. However, as a general precaution, consuming any unripe rowan berries can cause gastrointestinal issues due to their high content of sorbic acid.
